title: Thinking, Feelings, and Imagination Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A thought or plan in your mind.
choices: idea
imagine
reason
rounds.answer: idea
rounds.explanation: idea: A thought or plan in your mind.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A way to feel or understand something.
choices: idea
imagine
sense
rounds.answer: sense
rounds.explanation: sense: A way to feel or understand something.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Feeling like nothing will get better.
choices: imagine
hopeless
idea
rounds.answer: hopeless
rounds.explanation: hopeless: Feeling like nothing will get better.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To make a picture of something in your mind.
rounds.answer: imagine

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The feeling you have when you are unhappy.
rounds.answer: sadness

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.answer: enjoyable

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: I have an idea for a fun game we can play.
I have a reason for being late; my bus was slow.
She felt sadness when her friend moved away.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

choices: Feeling like nothing will get better.
Honest and truly meaning what you say.
Kind and caring about other people.
The feeling you have when you are unhappy.
rounds.answer: The feeling you have when you are unhappy.
rounds.explanation: She felt sadness when her friend moved away.

choices: Feeling like nothing will get better.
Fun or nice to do.
Kind and caring about other people.
Something that happens to you that you remember.
rounds.answer: Something that happens to you that you remember.
rounds.explanation: I had a fun experience at the zoo.

choices: Feeling like nothing will get better.
Fun or nice to do.
Honest and truly meaning what you say.
To let someone do something; to give permission.
rounds.answer: Honest and truly meaning what you say.
rounds.explanation: She gave me a sincere smile when I helped her.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What did the teacher bring to class? What can the children put inside it?
choices: Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.
A thought or plan in your mind.
To make a picture of something in your mind.
rounds.answer: Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.

rounds.prompt: What does the text say we should do when we feel sadness? Do you agree?
choices: A thought or plan in your mind.
To make a picture of something in your mind.
Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.
rounds.answer: Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.

rounds.prompt: What did the teacher bring to class, and what did she ask each child to put inside it?
choices: To make a picture of something in your mind.
Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.
A thought or plan in your mind.
rounds.answer: Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Accept an accurate complete-sentence response supported by a relevant detail from the selected text.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.target: I have an idea. I suggest we ______.
Imagine you can ______ (fly / talk to animals / be invisible).
Explain one important unit idea, give a reason with because, and respond to two follow-up questions.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: She gave me a sincere smile when I helped her.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Playing games with friends is very enjoyable.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I suggest we play outside after lunch.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'sadness' mean in this unit?
choices: The feeling you have when you are unhappy.
A thought or plan in your mind.
To make a picture of something in your mind.
rounds.answer: The feeling you have when you are unhappy.
rounds.explanation: sadness: The feeling you have when you are unhappy.

rounds.prompt: What does 'allow' mean in this unit?
choices: A thought or plan in your mind.
To make a picture of something in your mind.
To let someone do something; to give permission.
rounds.answer: To let someone do something; to give permission.
rounds.explanation: allow: To let someone do something; to give permission.

rounds.prompt: What does 'imagine' mean in this unit?
choices: The cause of something, or why it is done.
To make a picture of something in your mind.
A thought or plan in your mind.
rounds.answer: To make a picture of something in your mind.
rounds.explanation: imagine: To make a picture of something in your mind.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

choices: Adjective
Noun
Verb
rounds.answer: Adjective
rounds.explanation: thoughtful is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

choices: Noun
Adjective
Verb
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: enjoy is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

choices: Adjective
Noun
Verb
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: choice is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

choices: A way to feel or understand something.
Something that happens to you that you remember.
The feeling you have when you are unhappy.
The reason why something is made or done.
rounds.answer: The reason why something is made or done.
rounds.explanation: The purpose of a pencil is to help us write.

choices: A decision between two or more things.
Honest and truly meaning what you say.
Something that happens to you that you remember.
The feeling you have when you are unhappy.
rounds.answer: A decision between two or more things.
rounds.explanation: You can have a cookie or an apple; it is your choice.

choices: A way to feel or understand something.
Honest and truly meaning what you say.
Kind and caring about other people.
Something that happens to you that you remember.
rounds.answer: A way to feel or understand something.
rounds.explanation: I had a sense that it might rain soon.

games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

choices: A decision between two or more things.
A thought or plan in your mind.
The cause of something, or why it is done.
The reason why something is made or done.
rounds.answer: A thought or plan in your mind.
rounds.explanation: I have an idea for a fun game we can play.

choices: A decision between two or more things.
A way to feel or understand something.
The reason why something is made or done.
To make a picture of something in your mind.
rounds.answer: To make a picture of something in your mind.
rounds.explanation: I can imagine a big, colourful dragon flying in the sky.

choices: A decision between two or more things.
A way to feel or understand something.
The cause of something, or why it is done.
